concepts.title: Growth of Current in LR Circuit
concepts.conceptBody:
Growth of Current in LR Circuit

\[L\frac{di}{dt}\,\,\,+\,\,iR=\,\in \] 

 

$\Rightarrow \,\,\,\,\,\frac{\text{di}}{(\in -\text{iR})}=\left( \frac{1}{~\text{L}} \right)\text{dt}$ 

\[\Rightarrow \,\,\,\,\int_{{{i}_{0}}}^{{{i}_{8}}}{\frac{di}{(\in -iR)}}=\left( \frac{1}{L} \right)\int_{0}^{t}{d}t\] 

\[ln\frac{\left( \in -{{\text{i}}_{\text{s}}}\,\text{R} \right)}{\left( \in -{{\text{i}}_{0}}\,\text{R} \right)}=-\left( \frac{R}{L} \right)t\] 

\[\left( \in -\,{{i}_{s}}\,R \right)=\left( \in -\,\,{{i}_{0}}\,R \right){{e}^{-\frac{Rt}{L}}}\]

Time constant of LR circuit 

It is the time in which the current through an induction would acquire its steady state value, had it been increasing with initial constant rate.

 

 

$i(\tau )={{i}_{0}}+\left( {{i}_{s}}-{{i}_{0}} \right)0.63$

concepts.title: Decay of Current in LR Circuit
concepts.conceptBody:
Decay of Current in LR Circuit

$L\left( \frac{di}{dt} \right)+iR=0$ 

 

$\int_{i0}^{i(t)}{\frac{di}{i}}=-\frac{R}{L}\int_{0}^{\text{t}}{dt}$ 

$\frac{di}{dt}=\frac{-\text{i}}{\tau }{{\text{e}}^{-\frac{\text{Rt}}{\text{L}}}}$ 

Time constant is also defined as the time is which the current through an inductor would completely vanish, had it been decreasing with initial constant rate.

 

Tip : The time constant for LR circuit can also be found by the equivalent resistance across the terminals of inductor, just like in RC circuits.

concepts.title: Analysis of LR Circuit
concepts.conceptBody:
Analysis of LR Circuit 

L-R circuits can be treated as the converse of RC circuit's analysis.

1.At t = 0 

At t = 0, Replace the inductor with an open terminal (only when the current initially is zero).

Hence or otherwise use KVL and find the potential drop across the inductor. Thus, ${{i}_{0}}$ can be found

Now, to find the \[{{\left| \frac{di}{dt} \right|}_{t\,=\,0}}\], find the potential drop across the terminal of the inductor and use  $L\left( \frac{di}{dt} \right)=V$

2.At steady state : 

At steady state, the inductor is replaced by a short-circuit. Use KVL and find the current through the inductor, ${{i}_{s}}$

examples.title: In an $L R$-circuit, time constant is that time in which current grows from zero to the value (where $I_0$ is the steady state current)
